extra.reasoning: The output `acuity` is computed solely from rules r1–r8, none of which reference the input `stiff_neck`. The rules trigger on dizziness, abdominal_pain, palpitations, fever, syncope, vomiting, severe_headache, and slurred_speech. With priority-based conflict resolution (priority 3 → emergent beats priority 2 → urgent; default routine if nothing matches), the chosen acuity depends only on the truth values of those eight inputs and never on stiff_neck. Therefore, for any input assignment, flipping stiff_neck from false to true while holding all other inputs fixed leaves the set of matching rules unchanged, leaves the highest-priority winner unchanged, and leaves the default fallback unchanged — yielding an identical acuity. Since the output is equal (and thus "no lower"), the monotonicity property holds across the entire input space. No counterexample exists.
